crm_implementation.heading: Why CRM Implementations Fail
crm_implementation.subtitle: Most CRM projects fail because:
reasons: Systems are configured to match software defaults
Teams are excluded from early design decisions
Automation is added without understanding workflows
Data is migrated without structure or validation
Reporting is treated as an afterthought
crm_implementation.footerNote: We treat CRM implementation as an operational project, not just a technical one.

erp.heading: Why ERP Projects Fail
erp.subtitle: Most ERP initiatives fail for predictable reasons:
reasons: Lack of process mapping before implementation
erp.footerNote: We eliminate these risks by treating ERP as an operational system, not an IT deployment.

crm_customization.heading: When CRM Customisation Is Necessary
crm_customization.subtitle: CRM customisation becomes critical when:
reasons: Teams rely on spreadsheets alongside the CRM
crm_customization.footerNote: Customisation fixes these issues by aligning the system to real operations.

crm_integration.heading: When CRM Integration Is Critical
crm_integration.subtitle: CRM integration becomes essential when:
reasons: Sales, marketing, and finance disagree on metrics
Automation breaks due to missing data
crm_integration.footerNote: Integration fixes these problems at the system level.

crm_automation_ai.heading: When CRM Automation & AI Becomes Essential
crm_automation_ai.subtitle: This service is critical when:
reasons: Sales teams spend hours updating CRM manually
Follow ups are inconsistent or missed
crm_automation_ai.footerNote: Automation fixes the system, not just the symptoms.
